name: Using a mutable default value as an argument
description: Passing mutable lists or dictionaries as default arguments can have unforeseen consequences. The persistent object is reused across function calls.

name: No exception type(s) specified
description: Bare except clauses hide actual programming errors and lose information about the error.

name: Not using with to open files
description: Without 'with', files may not be properly closed if exceptions occur, leading to memory issues or file corruption.

name: Explicit return in __init__
description: __init__ should only initialize instance members, not return values.

name: Indentation contains mixed spaces and tabs
description: Python requires consistent indentation. Mixing spaces and tabs causes syntax errors and readability issues.

name: Not using get() to return a default value from a dict
description: Manual key checking is verbose. dict.get() provides cleaner default value handling.

name: Bad except clauses order
description: More specific exceptions must come before base classes, otherwise they become unreachable.

name: Not using defaultdict()
description: Manual dictionary initialization is verbose. defaultdict provides automatic default values.

name: Bad first argument to super()
description: super() requires the child class name as first argument and self as second for proper inheritance.

name: Method could be a function
description: Methods that don't use self or cls should be functions or static methods.

name: __exit__ must accept 3 arguments: type, value, traceback
description: Context managers must implement __exit__ with all required parameters (self, exc_type, exc_value, traceback).

name: Method has no argument
description: Instance methods must include 'self' as first parameter to access instance members.

name: __future__ import is not the first non-docstring statement
description: __future__ imports must be at the very top of the module to work correctly.

name: Implementing Java-style getters and setters
description: Python favors direct attribute access. Use properties only when computation is needed.

name: Assigning to built-in function
description: Overwriting built-in functions (like list, dict) breaks functionality elsewhere.

name: Accessing a protected member from outside the class
description: Protected members (_prefix) are intended for internal use only and may change.

name: Assigning a lambda expression to a variable
description: Lambdas are for anonymous functions. Use def for named functions.

name: Returning more than one variable type from function call
description: Functions should return consistent types. Use exceptions for error cases.

name: using wildcard imports (from … import *)
description: Wildcard imports pollute namespace and make code hard to trace.

name: Using single letter to name your variables
description: Single-letter variable names make code hard to understand and maintain.

name: Using the global statement
description: Global variables cause bugs from multiple functions accessing shared state.

name: Using CamelCase in function names
description: Function names should be lowercase with underscores per PEP 8.

name: Not using zip() to iterate over a pair of lists
description: Manual index iteration is verbose. zip() provides cleaner parallel iteration.

name: Not using unpacking for updating multiple values at once
description: Tuple unpacking is more concise and readable.

name: Test for object identity should be is
description: Use 'is' for None comparison, not '=='.

name: Using type() to compare types
description: isinstance() is preferred over type() for type checking.

name: Comparing things to True the wrong way
description: Explicit comparison to True is redundant.

name: use of exec
description: exec() executes arbitrary strings as code and is unsafe.

name: Not using dict keys when formatting strings
description: Using dictionary keys in format strings improves clarity.

name: Using key in list to check if key is contained in list
description: Lists are slow for membership checks compared to sets.

name: Not using items() to iterate over a dictionary
description: items() is more efficient and readable than key-based access.

name: else clause on loop without a break statement
description: Loop else executes even if no meaningful condition was met unless controlled by break.
